description: You are a quality engineer at a medical device manufacturer establishing statistical process control for a piston ring manufacturing process using the `pistonrings` dataset from the `qcc` package. Perform a complete SPC analysis including Phase I/II control charts, process capability analysis, and small shift detection. Using the `pistonrings` dataset (Samples 1-25 for Phase I calibration, Samples 26-40 for Phase II monitoring), create a multi-panel figure at `/home/ga/RProjects/output/spc_control_charts.png` showing X-bar and R charts (calibrated on Phase I, plotting all 40 samples) and a CUSUM chart (target=74.000, std.dev estimated from Phase I). Compute process capability indices (Cp, Cpk, Pp, Ppk) using Specification Limits LSL=73.950 and USL=74.050, saving the results to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/spc_capability.csv` with columns `metric` and `value`. Identify out-of-control points and save them to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/spc_ooc_points.csv` with columns `sample`, `chart_type`, `value`, and `violation`. Write the analysis script in `/home/ga/RProjects/spc_analysis.R`. Note that the `qcc` package must be installed to complete the task.
